_warm_on_start_comment: Pre-warm the LLM tiers at startup (core/runtime.py) so turn 1 doesn't pay the ~3s model cold-load on the user's first utterance (measured ttft 3.17s cold vs ~0.27s warm). Set false to measure cold start.

router._comment: threshold=0.3 (was 0.5): combined with the complexity + generation/long-form markers in core/routing.py, this lets reasoning and 'tell me a story' turns escalate from the fast tier to the main model instead of every turn answering on the small model (the field default scored 125/125 turns fast-only). Per-profile overrides (device_profiles.*.llm.router.threshold) still win. See docs/flow_control_intelligence_2026-05.md.

llm._live_routing_comment: smart-routing-2 + load follow-up + dynamic hedge (Task 4). OFF by default so behaviour is byte-identical. When true, the runtime feeds the router an ADDITIVE-ONLY, clamped live nudge toward main/cloud from (a) the recorder's rolling local TTFT EWMA and (b) a cheap core.sysinfo SystemMonitor load snapshot (reads the last background sample, no hot-path sampling), and -- for a cloud-racing HedgeLLM -- shortens the per-call hedge_delay_ms (clamped to a floor) so the cloud race starts sooner when local is slow/loaded. A missing/garbage signal is a no-op, so the local tier is never starved. Also honoured via SPEAKER_LIVE_ROUTING=1.

cloud._comment: Single-cloud back-compat: when cloud_providers/cloud_chains are absent, this block configures one cloud client. Set enabled=true and pick a base_url/model/api_key_env. For multi-provider sensitivity-routed chains, use llm.cloud_providers + llm.cloud_chains below and leave this block disabled. allow_prc gates the PRC-hosted (host=CN) presets -- off by default so US-hosted chains are the default; set true to opt in (Decision 2). timeout_s is a short cloud socket timeout (BR1) that reaps a losing worker still blocked in its first-token read fast. max_tokens is an optional per-turn output ceiling (BR4; null = unbounded, composed under each profile's cap). cost_order (smart-routing-5) is OFF by default; set true to stably reorder each cloud_chain's presets by ttft/$-per-Mtok metadata (fail-safe: same multiset, original order on any malformed input) so cheaper/faster providers race first.

cloud_providers._comment: Named OpenAI-compatible cloud LLM presets verified 2026-05-28. Each entry: base_url + model + api_key_env + profile (selects per-provider quirk handler in core/llm.py::PROVIDER_PROFILES) + pricing metadata. Selected from a chain in llm.cloud_chains.

openrouter_gpt_oss_120b._comment: OpenRouter (one key, many models; US-hosted aggregator). host=US so it survives the PRC opt-in gate by default (Decision 2). profile openai_compat is the safe generic shape.

cloud_chains._comment: Ordered failover chains by data-sensitivity. HedgeLLM tries the first cloud; on error/timeout it advances to the next; finally falls back to the local main LLM. US-only chains keep personal data under US legal jurisdiction; the public chain uses cheaper CN-hosted providers when the content isn't about the user.

cloud_routing._comment: Maps Sensitivity tag (from core/sensitivity.py) -> chain name above. default_chain is used when sensitivity is missing/unknown -- 'private' is the safe default.

desktop._comment: router.threshold=0.3 + the generation/long-form markers (core/routing.py) so reasoning + 'tell me a story' turns escalate to gemma3:12b (fast on this GPU, previously never used). num_predict=768 gives long-form answers room (a story isn't 384 tokens). asr_num_threads=2: a small streaming zipformer doesn't speed up past 2 CPU threads. input_gate ON (core/addressing.py): the fast tier has headroom here, so gate ambient/read-aloud speech instead of answering disfluency fragments. cleanup left OFF -- it adds a second fast-LLM call on the capture thread per final (see docs/perf_audit_2026-05_goal_alignment.md H8). See docs/flow_control_intelligence_2026-05.md.

desktop_gpu_4090._comment: RTX 4090 / 5090 / equivalent: gemma3:12b on GPU + gemma3:4b fast tier; both gates ON (fast tier has plenty of headroom). Specsim quick TTFA 0.96s. Cloud chains off by default -- local 12b is fast enough; enable in config.local.json for paragraph-length research-mode answers.

macbook_m_series._comment: Apple Silicon (M2/M3/M4 + 16GB+ unified RAM): gemma3:4b main on Metal + gemma3:1b fast. Both gates ON. Specsim quick TTFA 1.46s. Sensitivity-routed cloud chains ENABLED (hedge strategy): private turns -> Cerebras+Groq (US); code turns -> Cerebras Coder+Kimi; public turns -> DeepSeek-Flash (CN, cheap). Provide the four API keys via env to activate; missing keys disable that chain individually.

cpu_laptop._comment: Mid-range Windows/Linux laptop, no dedicated GPU (8 cores, 16GB). Local gemma3:1b only -- the 4b main is too slow on pure CPU (specsim quick TTFA would be ~3.5s+). Sensitivity-routed cloud chains ENABLED with hedge_delay_ms=0 (kick cloud immediately, don't wait for local). Both gates OFF (no fast-tier headroom on CPU).

phone._comment: router.threshold=0.55 overrides the base 0.3: on phone the 'main' tier is gemma-3-4b GGUF on CPU (n_gpu_layers=0), so over-escalating idiom/borderline turns onto it is a real latency hit. Keep escalation reserved for clearly heavy turns here (the doc-prescribed phone safety valve, docs/flow_control_intelligence_2026-05.md).

phone_lite._comment: Low-end phone (6GB RAM, mid-range SoC): single-tier gemma3:1b GGUF Q4 only. The 4b model thrashes RAM at this tier. Cloud chains ENABLED with fallback strategy -- cloud-first (with ttft_deadline), local-only as last resort. Specsim quick TTFA on-device 5s; with cloud hedge 1-2s. Gates OFF.

input_gate._comment: Optional ACT/INGEST gate (core/addressing.py). Disabled by default; enable to stop the assistant from answering ambient speech / read-aloud text. Needs llm.fast_model. unsure_acts=true preserves prior behavior; false is conservative (silently ingest).

cleanup._comment: Optional ASR transcript cleanup (core/cleanup.py). Disabled by default; enable to rewrite the raw final into the user's intended sentence -- drops fillers, resolves self-corrections, handles the word-repeat-at-end correction signal. Needs llm.fast_model. Adds one fast-tier LLM call per ACT'd final.

continuation._comment: ADD-ON / continuation: when a follow-up extends the in-flight assistant turn ('and also...', 'oh wait make it shorter', 'what about Mars too'), merge it into one coherent answer instead of racing a competing cold task. Before the turn starts speaking the two prompts are merged into one fresh turn; after it's already speaking the add-on is answered as a context-carrying continuation queued behind it (no re-speak, no overlap). Deterministic + local (no LLM on this path). markers/trailing_markers/modifier_leads override the built-in EN/RO cue sets; addon_max_words bounds the short-modifier branch.

assistant._comment: Optional persona so the model knows WHAT IT IS. Its SKILLS are enumerated automatically from the live capability manifest (always_on_agent/capabilities.py specs) into the system prompt, and the web-access line reflects the real web_search.enabled / §9.7 egress state. Leave name empty for the anonymous 'local voice assistant' identity; set name (and optionally a one-line persona / extra guidance) to give it a character.

planner._comment: enabled=true makes the ReAct planner reachable: ASSISTANT-mode queries that hit an _ESCALATE_MARKER (search/find/compare/'and then'/step by step -- see always_on_agent/react.py) run a bounded plan->execute loop over the tools below instead of degrading to a one-shot reply. Gated, so ordinary short turns stay one-shot. web.search returns corpus-only results until web_search.enabled=true (egress-gated, §9.7). See docs/flow_control_intelligence_2026-05.md.

web_search._comment: FLAT web.search config (P3, Decision 3). Disabled by default: the runtime ships corpus-only (search.local). To enable real web research, set enabled=true and base_url to your self-hosted SearXNG (keyless). Every query is routed through the §9.7 egress gate (core.sensitivity.may_leave_device) BEFORE any network call -- PII/personal queries never egress (corpus only). timeout_s bounds the connect+read GET (BR7; the only wedge bound since a blocking GET can't poll cancel). max_results caps mapped hits.

sherpa._endpoint_comment: Semantic (turn-completion) endpointing layered on the acoustic rule2 timer (core/endpointing.py). A turn-completion detector + adaptive policy commit a final EARLY when the partial reads as a complete turn (down to endpoint_min_silence_sec) and HOLD past rule2 (up to endpoint_max_silence_sec) when it ends mid-phrase ('...and', '...the'). ENABLED 2026-05-30 after an on-device A/B (docs/live_validation_run_2026-05-30.md): it reclaims the dominant ~1.2s trailing-silence wait -> ~0.9s endpoint, ~300ms first-audio win, with NO tail clipping and NO sentence splitting across all scenarios. endpoint_min_silence_sec=0.7 (raised from 0.5) is the validated floor: it must exceed BOTH the decoder lookahead (or an early commit clips the last word) AND a typical intra-sentence comma pause (at 0.5 a run-on like 'Hey, what are you, and...' split at the comma; 0.7 fixed the split while keeping the win). Re-validate per deployment with `python -m tools.live_session --all --inject --smart-endpoint` and diff the ON finals against the acoustic (OFF) finals before trusting a new value/model. The shipped detector is lexical; a Smart Turn v3 audio (prosody) model drops in via the same seam.
sherpa._endpoint_high_confidence_comment: Adaptive confidence-tiered SHORTEN floor (2026-06-01). A HIGH-confidence completion (lexical 0.75 bin -- a normal ending word, NEVER a conjunction/article/filler) commits at endpoint_high_confidence_floor (0.6s) instead of the full endpoint_min_silence_sec (0.7s), reclaiming ~110ms endpoint p50 / first-audio on the common well-formed-turn case. VALIDATED on-device 2026-06-01: floor=0.6 cut endpoint p50 918->806ms with text-diffs EQUAL to the inject run-to-run variance floor (6 vs 6 -- no extra splits/truncations) and the 'In one sentence, how do plants...' comma run-on stayed INTACT; floor=0.55 truncated it (13 diffs, 2x variance) and was rejected. 0.6 still exceeds the decoder lookahead AND a typical comma pause. A medium-confidence complete keeps the full 0.7 floor. Set endpoint_high_confidence_floor=0 to revert to the uniform floor. A premature commit is merged back by the continuation layer. Re-validate with `python -m tools.live_session --scenario latency_profile_mixed --scenario self_awareness_enumerate_do_decline --inject --endpoint-high-confidence-floor 0.6` and diff the finals vs a floor-0 run.
sherpa._endpoint_detector_comment: Turn-completion detector: 'lexical' (default, text-only, cheap) or 'prosody' (Smart Turn v3 audio model -- reads the rising/sustained intonation of a mid-thought trailing-off that lexical can't, so the floor can drop further). Prosody needs endpoint_prosody_model (download: python -m tools.setup_models --turn-model). The completion_score becomes the model's P(complete), so the SAME adaptive policy applies (>=0.75 -> high_confidence_floor; <=0.3 -> EXTEND/hold). VALIDATED on the user's REAL voice 2026-06-01 (tools/turn_detect_check): complete 0.74-0.98 vs incomplete 0.01-0.56, margin 0.18 -- but the model is HUMAN-AUDIO ONLY (flat ~0.97 on TTS), so it CANNOT be validated with the synthetic-user/inject harness; validate the live floor-lowering on real speech (python -m core --engine sherpa, then read logs/runs/*.summary.json endpoint latency + transcripts). endpoint_prosody_min_silence gates the ~15ms model to the trailing-silence window (not every block). Default detector stays 'lexical' (byte-identical) until the live floor-lowering is validated.
sherpa._denoise_comment: Optional speech denoiser (sherpa-onnx GTCRN) on the 16 kHz capture block, applied right after resampling and BEFORE the recognizer, the speaker embedder, and the VAD all read it. Broadband noise both garbles STT and drags the enrolled-user speaker embedding below the gate (0.30-0.46 vs the 0.5 threshold -> lockout); one denoise cleans the single block for every downstream model. OFF by default: denoise_enabled=false OR denoise_model='' builds NOTHING and the capture path is byte-identical to no-denoise (gate mirrors endpoint_enabled). Fetch the 523KB model with `python -m tools.setup_models --denoise-model` (wires denoise_model into config.local.json), then set denoise_enabled=true. Costs one tiny ONNX inference per 0.1s block on the capture thread (GTCRN RTF<<1; measure with tools.noise_stress before enabling on weak tiers). NOTE: the recorder writes the DENOISED block, so a recording made with denoise ON replays already-denoised -- keep this flag consistent across record/replay or you double-denoise. Re-enroll your voice after enabling (the embedding shifts vs an un-denoised enrollment).

memory._comment: FLAT P2 memory seam config (R10) -- single-level so a device-profile override survives the shallow merge. backend: auto|inmemory|postgres (auto picks Postgres only when $DATABASE_URL is set). recall_enabled is OFF by default: recall must be measured (tools.bench) before enabling. P2b (live, Postgres-only): profile_enabled gates the default-OFF user-profile producer (deterministic regex at ingest + optional fast-LLM pass on the writer thread); episodic_ttl_days/summary_ttl_days are the age TTLs apply_retention() enforces at close-time -- episodic 'messages' are summarized-then-evicted past episodic_ttl_days, 'summaries' dropped past summary_ttl_days, user_profile never TTL'd. The rolling summary uses the fast LLM off the bus thread.
memory._recent_context_comment: Short-term conversation memory: inject the last recent_context_turns user/assistant turns into the answering model's prompt so it can resolve references ('its', 'the second one', 'make it shorter') to what was just said. Cheap (no embeddings, unlike recall_* above which surfaces relevant PAST-session snippets). Default ON; bounded by turns + chars.
